# Systematic Uncertainties

A realistic analysis is almost never limited to purely statistical fluctuations.
One needs a clean way to introduce, constrain, and propagate systematic effects so
that they enter the model transparently and remain testable.

The structural outline is straightforward: nuisance parameters, covariance matrices,
constrained fits, and simple propagation schemes.

::: {.notebook-card}
### Companion notebook

The notebook for this chapter shows the simplest combination of statistical and
systematic uncertainties and leaves room for more serious covariance studies later.

<div class="notebook-actions">
  <a class="notebook-link notebook-link-primary" href="../notebooks/systematics_demo.qmd">Open notebook</a>
</div>
:::
